children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: Not later than , and July 1 of each year thereafter, the Secretary shall submit to Congress a comprehensive and detailed annual report on the activities of the Secretary under this chapter during the most recently completed fiscal year.July 1, 2000
include a list of all grant recipients by State, including the projected private sector dollar to Federal dollar investment ratio for each grant recipient;

children.label: A
children.text: rural areas and urban economically distressed areas; and
after the completion of a project, include the realized private sector dollar to Federal dollar investment ratio for the project; and

children.label: A
children.text: include a list of all of the grants provided by the Economic Development Administration for projects located in, or that primarily benefit, rural areas;

children.label: B
children.text: an explanation of the process used to determine how each project referred to in subparagraph (A) would benefit a rural area; and

children.label: i
children.text: is located in a rural area; or

children.label: ii
children.text: will primarily benefit a rural area.
The award date of the project.
The completion date of the project.
The close out date of the project.
The total amount of the project, including non-Federal cost share and funding from other sources, including a breakdown by source.
The number of jobs anticipated to be created or retained as a result of the investment.

children.heading: Public availability
children.label: d Public availability
children.text: Not later than the date of the submission of the report under subsection (c), the Secretary shall make the report under subsection (c) publicly available.
includes an analysis of Economic Development Administration construction project timeline estimates and actual project durations; and
describes the frequency with which project timelines are delayed and the sources of those delays, including cases in which a project scope or schedule requires an award amendment.
